element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Button mashing fundamentals; computer opponents break records at Olympic difficulty
Timing-based variant of sprint mechanic; noted as challenging
Difficulty spike; computer competitive even at lower levels
Player control over jump execution; mastery allows clearing 6.35m+ through practice
Slow-paced button input; country-specific strengths (Italians dominant)
Full player control over jump selection and execution; reaches 0.0 or 9.0+ through skill, not predefined sequences

typical_relief_triggers: Country-specific athlete strengths matching event (Italian swimmer, German archer)
typical_tension_triggers: Sprint events requiring sustained button mashing
Computer opponents breaking records at Olympic difficulty
Diving/pole vault requiring precise player control without safety net
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Event-based rhythm; intensity varies by discipline type. Mechanics alternate between button-mashing tension (sprints/hurdles) and precision-control tension (diving/pole vault), with relief via country-advantage events. Difficulty scales discretely across club/national/Olympic modes.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Club difficulty: computer athletes achieve mediocre results; accessible for new players
difficulty_curve.mid_game: National difficulty: noticeable challenge increase; computer begins competitive record-breaking
difficulty_curve.late_game: Olympic difficulty: computer athletes frequently break world and Olympic records; sprinting, hurdles, and hammer become substantially harder; relies on player skill and random variance
notable_spikes: Sprinting events at Olympic difficulty (button-mashing endurance + record-breaking AI)
Hurdles at Olympic difficulty (timing precision + AI competitiveness)
Hammer throw across all difficulties (noted as inherently challenging)

notable_level_patterns.description: Players can practice individual events, play mini-Olympics (selectable event subset), or full Olympics. This scaffolding allows learning events before competition pressure.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Practice mode for any event; mini-Olympics with hammer/hurdles disabled

notable_level_patterns.description: Each nation excels at specific events (Germans/archery, Italians/swimming, Russians/pole vault, Americans/sprinting). Computer opponents perform better or worse depending on event-country pairing.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any competition featuring Italian athletes in freestyle swimming or German archers

notable_level_patterns.description: Core gameplay shifts: button-mashing (sprints/hurdles/hammer), slow-paced input (swimming), precision control (diving/pole vault), non-button-mashing (archery). Creates rhythm variation across competition.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Full Olympics sequence cycling through all event types

notable_level_patterns.description: Three discrete difficulty tiers affect opponent performance intensity. Club = baseline, National = moderate challenge, Olympic = record-breaking AI with random variance. Creates replay value across skill levels.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Same event (e.g., 100m) replayed at club vs. Olympic difficulty
